key_insight: Data-efficient fine-tuning of a MACE foundation model can recover the barocaloric phase transformation of ammonium sulfate with only 5–10 DFT configurations.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a practical route to screen and model barocaloric cooling materials using far fewer expensive quantum-mechanical reference calculations.
method: Train MACE MLIPs for ammonium sulfate using three strategies: from scratch, naive replay fine-tuning, and multihead replay fine-tuning.
Evaluate each model in molecular dynamics simulations across multiple training-set sizes.
Test whether the models reproduce the temperature-driven structural phase transformation and assess transfer to hybrid-DFT/dispersion-corrected data.
evidence_signal: Fine-tuned models reproduced the phase transformation with as few as 5–10 60-atom DFT configurations, while the foundation model failed and scratch-trained models broke down at small data sizes.
watch_item: The result depends on including dispersion corrections and may not transfer equally well outside the fine-tuning domain or to other barocaloric materials.
paper_title: Barocaloric phase transformation from data efficient fine-tuning of machine learned interatomic potentials
